name: Dinner Crepes with Mushroom and Walnut Fillin
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the sauce by heating 1 tablespoon oil in a small saucepan. Add the flour and whisk while cooking for 1 minute. Remove from heat and pour in the hot soymilk all at once. Whisk briskly and return to heat. Bring to a boil, continuing to whisk, and cook for 2 more minutes or until thickened. If lumps appear, beat with an eggbeater until smooth. Reserve 1/4 cup of white sauce for filling; combine the rest with tomato sauce and reheat before serving.
2. Prepare the filling by heating 2 tablespoons oil in a large skillet. Add mushrooms, garlic, thyme, basil, salt, and pepper. Mix well, cover, and cook for 20 minutes. Remove cover, add walnuts, and cook a few more minutes to absorb liquid. Combine with reserved white sauce.
3. Prepare the crepes last. Sift together flour and salt. Mix 2 tablespoons oil and soymilk. Pour soymilk mixture into flour and mix well. If small lumps remain, beat for 30 seconds. Heat an 8-inch nonstick skillet or coat a covered skillet with lecithin. When a drop of water sizzles, the skillet is ready. Pour batter to coat bottom completely; cook about 1 minute until golden brown. Loosen edges, turn over, and cook another minute. Repeat until all batter is used. Stack crepes for later use.
4. Lay filling down center of each crepe and roll up. Place side by side in serving dish, cover with warm sauce, and serve.
